problems.title: Bakery Production Mix
problems.scenario: A small bakery makes two products: muffins and cookies. Each muffin earns $3 profit and requires 2 cups of flour and 1 egg. Each cookie earns $2 profit and requires 1 cup of flour and 2 eggs. The bakery has 100 cups of flour and 80 eggs available today. How many of each should they make to maximize profit?

problems.title: Minimum Cost Diet
problems.scenario: A nutritionist is designing a minimum-cost daily meal using two foods. Food A costs $4 per serving and provides 3 units of protein and 2 units of vitamins. Food B costs $5 per serving and provides 2 units of protein and 3 units of vitamins. The meal must contain at least 12 units of protein and at least 12 units of vitamins. How many servings of each minimize cost?

problems.title: Three-Product Factory
problems.scenario: A factory produces three products. Profits per unit are $5, $4, and $3. Each product uses machine time, labor, and raw material. Product 1 uses 2 hours of machine time, 1 hour of labor, and 1 unit of material. Product 2 uses 1, 2, and 1. Product 3 uses 1, 1, and 2. The factory has 40 machine hours, 30 labor hours, and 35 units of material. Maximize profit.
